fixtures.notes: Single PR bundles a performance optimisation (dedup schema dumps) with an unrelated internal refactoring of dump_schema to use with_temporary_pool, and separately refactors the test helper include.
PR title explicitly says "Refactor READ_QUERY AND fix missing arguments" — a structural refactor (extracting DEFAULT_READ_QUERY) is bundled with a bug fix (adding missing SQL keywords per adapter).

A single PR bundles two distinct concerns: a bug fix to SpringBeanContainer.java (removing incorrect name-to-type delegation, adding missing autowireBeanProperties call) alongside adding a brand-new 344-line integration test file.

Three unrelated concerns bundled together: (1) correcting a broken --frozen flag to --frozen-lockfile, (2) adding a new post-install lockfile integrity check, and (3) bumping dozens of yarn.lock dependency versions.
Schema-dedupe behavior changes are bundled with unrelated test-helper include churn in the same PR, increasing scope beyond a single cohesive objective.
Container resolution behavior changes by removing a fallback path while the method contract still appears to support resilient retrieval semantics.
A lockfile-integrity intent is introduced, but the same change set bundles broad dependency churn and build-path edits that dilute the stated objective and reviewability.
The worker keeps retry semantics but removes structured failure logging, causing behavior to diverge from the apparent operational intent of actionable retries.
Provisioning still returns errors, but key error-path diagnostics are removed, conflicting with the apparent intent to preserve debuggable failure behavior.
Logging moves from formatted strings to structured fields without changing return paths, so implementation remains aligned with the existing failure-handling intent.
Named bean retrieval fallback is removed while signatures stay the same, materially changing runtime resolution behavior.
Async polling flow is converted to synchronous blocking plus broad exception handling, changing scheduling and failure behavior at runtime.
fixtures.sample_finding: Blocking async/synchronization pattern was introduced in changed code.
fixtures.sample_evidence: signal=DET-CONC-001; unknown:970 time.sleep(poll_interval)

fixtures.notes: Per-battle lock removal allows message handlers to interleave and changes state transition behavior under concurrency.
Per-battle lock addition preserves intended state sequencing and prevents races; behavior remains aligned with expected processing semantics.
Process stream handling is improved to read stdout/stderr concurrently, but outer call still synchronously blocks on completion, making behavioral impact nuanced.
fixtures.sample_finding: Blocking async/synchronization pattern was introduced in changed code.
fixtures.sample_evidence: signal=DET-CONC-001; unknown:143 var results = Task.WhenAll(outputTask, errorTask).GetAwaiter().GetResult();

fixtures.notes: Logging API is refactored from Errorf to ErrorS while method returns and external behavior stay contract-compatible.
SQL alias quoting semantics change and release notes call out raw SQL alias adjustments, creating caller-integration contract risk.
Existing quote_name_unless_alias path remains available as a deprecation shim that forwards to quote_name, preserving compatibility.
A new integration test is added, but it only validates a happy-path bean retrieval and does not assert the changed missing-bean-name fallback behavior or new property autowire branch.
Core dump_all behavior is changed to dedupe schemas across configs, but the test delta is only helper include churn and adds no assertions for the new branch behavior.

dump_schema execution is refactored to use with_temporary_pool and connection-scoped schema version writes, while test changes remain setup-only with no assertion coverage for the altered execution path.
Provisioning failure-path behavior and diagnostics are changed, but no corresponding test assertions are added to validate the modified error path.
Retry-adjacent rescue-path diagnostics are removed without adding tests that assert the changed failure-observability behavior.
A fallback branch for unresolved named beans is removed, increasing edge-path fragility when name-based lookup misses at runtime.
Operational error context is stripped from a failure path, reducing boundary-case diagnosability when provisioning fails in production.
Retry behavior remains, but request and backtrace context are removed from rescue logging, creating a fragile edge condition under repeated failures.
Error logging API style changes, but all guard/return behavior remains intact, so edge-case handling semantics are preserved.
Explicitly disabling parallel updates narrows a concurrency edge case rather than introducing a new invalid-input or boundary-risk path.

fixtures.notes: Critical provisioning error logs are removed on the failure path, leaving only a raw return/propagation with reduced diagnostics for failed jobs.
Structured rescue-block logging is removed before re-raising, which drops request/account context needed to diagnose retrying job failures.
Catch blocks continue throwing wrapped exceptions, but removal of explicit catch-path logs loses local failure context and weakens incident triage.
Error logging is added to previously silent provisioning failure paths, improving diagnostics and preserving error visibility.
Error calls are refactored from unstructured to structured logging APIs while preserving returns and emitted error signals.
A small tooling correction is bundled with broad lockfile churn and new bypass controls, creating disproportionate review complexity for the stated task.
A targeted container bugfix is mixed with a large new integration-test payload in the same change set, increasing cognitive load beyond the core fix.
Error-path observability behavior is altered across multiple sites, increasing troubleshooting complexity while not simplifying the core workflow.
Changing debug logs to info increases visibility but does not add structural branching or algorithmic complexity.
Setting PARALLEL_UPDATES to zero reduces concurrent state surface area and simplifies execution semantics in an edge deployment path.

Migration from Errorf to ErrorS standardizes logging style while preserving existing control flow and guard behavior.

fixtures.notes: Apple Developer Team ID hardcoded as environment variable fallback, embedding it in source.
Docker Compose uses ${VAR:-default} substitution so credentials come from the host environment at runtime.
A runtime message check is refactored to a constant without introducing secrets or brittle fallback defaults.
Routes reloader wiring replaces a hardcoded watcher choice with a configured file_watcher value.
Admin test expectations switch from a literal primary key to a fixture-driven value, removing hardcoded test data.
Single-row SELECT issued per contact inside a loop over all contacts, causing O(n) database queries.
Dataset SAV file downloaded from S3 on every API request with no caching, causing repeated heavy I/O.
Contacts fetched in batches via IN query before the loop; no per-item database roundtrip occurs.
Deferred-field guard prevents per-row related lookups from triggering unexpected N+1 query patterns.
Default watch cache sizing disables event watch cache paths, increasing hot-path API load risk.
Parameterized SQL is replaced with string interpolation, opening a direct SQL injection path for user-influenced input.
Change adds SQL pattern blocking and restores exception handling around query execution, reducing injection risk.
Remote calendar client now accepts a user-provided verify_ssl flag, enabling insecure TLS behavior when disabled.
Identifier generation switches to strict quote_name usage, hardening SQL compilation behavior rather than weakening it.
Admin-side selectors now escape dynamic fragments with CSS.escape, reducing selector injection surface.
Error log statement is removed from the HTTP handler error path, silencing failures during provisioning job processing.
The requestID correlation field is dropped from the error log, making it impossible to trace failed requests in distributed logs.
Structured logger with requestId context is replaced by console.log on a critical payment processing path, losing correlation metadata.
Full structured error log including request_id and backtrace is removed from the rescue block, swallowing exception context silently.
Error log with trace_id correlation is removed from the span processing error handler, eliminating observability into pipeline failures.
Structured error log with document ID is removed from the catch block, silently discarding indexing failure details.
Error logging is ADDED to a previously silent error handler, improving observability rather than degrading it.
Error log is refactored from unstructured klog.Errorf to structured klog.ErrorS, but error logging remains present on the error path.
Log level is upgraded from DEBUG to INFO on a scrape path, improving observability visibility rather than removing or degrading logging.
Only a noisy debug log in a hot middleware loop is removed; the error-level rescue log with request context is preserved, so observability is not degraded.
Schema drops a column and re-adds it in Down, but prior data is irreversibly lost after rollback.

Removing per-battle locking allows concurrent handlers to interleave updates for the same battle_tag, risking state corruption and lost transitions.
Per-battle lock initialization and guarded handling are added back, serializing updates and preserving state integrity under concurrent message delivery.
The migration is made explicitly irreversible on down, which fails fast instead of pretending dropped relational data can be recreated safely.

fixtures.notes: Concurrent battle message handlers run without any asyncio.Lock, so two tasks for the same battle can interleave state updates — classic async race condition.
Introduces per-battle asyncio.Lock objects to serialize concurrent message handlers — this is the correct thread-safe fix.
GetAwaiter().GetResult() blocks the calling thread, but the overall fix reads stdout and stderr concurrently via Task.WhenAll — debatable: avoids the pipe deadlock but still blocks the outer thread.

fixtures.notes: Shared modbus client call path removes asyncio.Lock serialization, increasing concurrent request interleaving risk.
Speaker grouping is applied incrementally with per-step waits, explicitly reducing topology race hazards.
SQL query built via string interpolation (injection), user-supplied input passed directly to the database, and no error handling — three compounding high-severity signals with no tests changed.

Blocking HTTP call in async init() stalls the event loop; no timeout on the request; no error handling around the JSON parse; and zero test coverage of the change — four high-severity signals stacked together.

Removes per-battle locking (race condition reintroduced), adds a shared mutable dict pop() call outside the lock, and removes battle_tag cleanup in the finish handler — three interacting state-corruption vectors with no test changes.

Converts async polling function to sync (blocking event loop), uses broad except to swallow all errors including non-transient failures, and removes asyncio.to_thread safety wrapper — three high-severity signals: blocking I/O, silent error swallowing, and behavioral regression.
